destinations.evidence: The public fee surface exports only the model-specific LLM deposit helper, not the removed default estimate helper.
LLM pricing fails when output/model/registry model information is missing instead of falling back to hardcoded model parameters.

equivalence_argument: The port has no exported default LLM executor estimate path; runtime fee calculation and wallet deposit both require model-specific registry data, with explicit errors for missing output or model information.
notes: Local searches found the model-specific helper and no LLM_DEFAULT fallback constants or default estimate function.
rationale: The commit body says fallback pricing patterns are removed and pricing errors must be surfaced; the diff removes LLM_DEFAULT_PARAMS_B, LLM_DEFAULT_SEQ_LEN, and the deprecated llm_executor_deposit_estimate export/function. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async fee calculation no longer exposes or uses a default LLM executor deposit estimate based on hardcoded fallback model parameters; callers must use model-specific LLM pricing via llm_executor_deposit_estimate_for_model.
suggested_falsification_tests: Attempt to price an LLM call without extracting a model or without registry model info and assert the builder/verifier receives an error rather than silently using hardcoded default parameter and sequence-length pricing.
summary: LLM pricing uses model-specific registry data without a default fallback estimate.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Attempt to price an LLM call without extracting a model or without registry model info and assert the builder/verifier receives an error rather than silently using hardcoded default parameter and sequence-length pricing.
title: LLM pricing has no default fallback estimate
